excerpt:
<details open>

llama : make tensor-split regex patterns static (#24710)

llama_meta_device_get_split_state() recompiled 29 std::regex on every call.
In -sm tensor mode the callback runs once per tensor per token, so this
dominated the decode thread in profiling. Mark them static const so they are
compiled once. Kept inside the function (local statics are thread-safe since
C++11). Patterns are literal and stateless, so behavior is unchanged.

</details>

**macOS/iOS:**
- [macOS Apple Silicon (arm64)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9966/llama-b9966-bin-macos-arm64.tar.gz)
- macOS Apple Silicon (arm64, KleidiAI enabled) [DISABLED](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/23780)
- [macOS Intel (x64)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9966/llama-b9966-bin-macos-x64.tar.gz)
- [iOS XCFramework](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9966/llama-b9966-xcframework.zip)

**Linux:**
- [Ubuntu x64 (CPU)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9966/llama-b9966-bin-ubuntu-x64.tar.gz)
- [Ubuntu arm64 (CPU)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9966/llama-b9966-bin-ubuntu-arm64.tar.gz)
- [Ubu
